steps.title: Create and test the first graduate futures evidence pack
steps.summary: This step creates the first working version of the evidence pack that programme teams will use to understand their students' graduate futures. It starts with the evidence itself: what we can include, what needs careful interpretation, and where the data has limits. It then tests whether the pack helps colleagues see a fair and useful picture of what their programme enables for students.

outputs.title: Data specification
outputs.summary: A clear specification of the sources, fields, owners, caveats, update cycles, suppression rules, segmentation and responsible-use principles for the first pack.

outputs.title: Interpretation approach
outputs.summary: An agreed approach to interpreting destinations, outcomes, comparator evidence, student aspiration data and programme context.

outputs.title: First working graduate futures evidence pack
outputs.summary: A first visual and narrative pack, including core data visualisations and explanatory material for testing with colleagues.

outputs.title: Early user feedback
outputs.summary: Feedback from selected academic and professional services users on whether the pack is understandable, credible, fair and useful.

outputs.title: Improvement list for the next version
outputs.summary: A short, prioritised list of changes needed before the pack is shared more widely.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide employability data expertise, destination interpretation and responsible-use guidance.
Support data access, specification, caveats and data quality checks.

existingCapacity.role: Power BI or equivalent visualisation support
existingCapacity.contribution: Help turn the specification into a usable first visual pack.
Test whether the first pack is clear, fair and useful from a programme perspective.

existingCapacity.role: Mark Peace, Daniel Robson and Careers colleagues
existingCapacity.contribution: Shape the evidence pack, interpretation approach and testing questions.

cashCosts.rationale: Approximate cost including on-costs for a student placement to support data preparation, visualisation development and early testing across Steps 1 and 2.

newInvestment.rationale: Modest dedicated capacity to support data preparation, dashboard or pack development, visualisation quality and user testing.

enablingConditions.condition: Access to the relevant graduate outcomes, destinations, aspiration, comparator and programme context data.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The first pack cannot provide a credible programme-level view.

enablingConditions.condition: Agreement on responsible use, caveats and small-number handling.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pack could look more definitive than the evidence allows.

decisions.title: Confirm evidence for the first version
decisions.summary: Agree what evidence is robust enough to include and what should be held back because it is incomplete, misleading or too difficult to interpret responsibly.

decisions.title: Confirm early testers
decisions.summary: Agree which programme, department, faculty and professional services colleagues should test the first version.

decisions.title: Confirm caveats and interpretation guidance
decisions.summary: Agree what explanation must sit alongside the data so colleagues can interpret it responsibly.

decisions.title: Confirm September 2026 scope
decisions.summary: Agree what the pack needs to do by September 2026 and what can wait for later versions.

decisions.title: Confirm summer placement funding
decisions.summary: Agree the approximately £3,000 student placement investment that supports data and visualisation work across Steps 1 and 2.

risks.title: The first pack looks too definitive
risks.mitigation: Use visible caveats, clear development status and testing language.

risks.title: Comparator evidence encourages league-table thinking
risks.mitigation: Frame comparators as context for reflection, not ranking.

risks.title: Uncertainty is hidden
risks.mitigation: Make data gaps, small numbers, suppression and volatility visible.

risks.title: Testing misses the academic user perspective
risks.mitigation: Include colleagues who will actually need to use the pack in programme conversations.

steps.title: Share the first programme packs and learn from use
steps.summary: This step shares the first programme-facing packs with colleagues and learns from how they use them. The aim is to see whether the packs help programme teams recognise strengths, ask better questions, and describe their students' graduate futures with more confidence. It should also show where the evidence, language or guidance needs to be improved.

outputs.title: Second version of the programme evidence packs
outputs.summary: Programme-facing packs improved from the first round of testing.

outputs.title: Short user guide
outputs.summary: A concise guide to help colleagues read and use the packs responsibly.

outputs.title: Faculty conversations
outputs.summary: Faculty-level discussions about what the evidence is showing, where it is useful and where it needs care.

outputs.title: Early institutional summary
outputs.summary: A short summary for Education Committee or the agreed senior education forum, drawing out early themes and questions.

outputs.title: Learning from use
outputs.summary: A record of what colleagues find useful, confusing, sensitive or missing, to inform the next version of the packs and the guided programme conversations.

existingCapacity.contribution: Produce the second version of the packs and respond to issues from early testing.
Support interpretation and responsible-use guidance.
Host and shape local conversations about the packs.
Bring together learning from use and prepare the early institutional summary.

newInvestment.item: Shared use of the Step 1 summer student placement
newInvestment.rationale: The same placement cost covers support for the first evidence pack, the second programme pack version, data preparation, visualisation development and early testing.
newInvestment.additionalCost: No additional cost beyond the approximately £3,000 identified in Step 1.

nonCashNeeds.rationale: Needed to keep the packs and user guide clear, concise and accessible rather than turning them into technical manuals.

decisions.title: Confirm sharing route
decisions.summary: Agree how the first programme packs should be shared and with whom.

decisions.title: Confirm faculty and institutional conversations
decisions.summary: Agree which faculty or institutional conversations need to happen before wider use.

decisions.title: Confirm senior forum
decisions.summary: Agree whether Education Committee or another senior education forum should receive the early institutional summary.

decisions.title: Confirm interpretation level
decisions.summary: Agree how much interpretation should be included in the packs themselves.

decisions.title: Confirm handling of sensitive findings
decisions.summary: Agree how sensitive or surprising findings should be discussed without creating anxiety, defensiveness or premature conclusions.

risks.title: Sharing feels like performance reporting
risks.mitigation: Use developmental framing and make clear that the packs are intended to help colleagues understand and evidence their graduate futures story.

risks.title: The packs are useful only with heavy explanation
risks.mitigation: Use a short guide and test whether colleagues can understand the core messages directly.

risks.title: Christmas 2026 becomes overloaded
risks.mitigation: Keep the scope focused and separate essential outputs from improvements that can wait.

steps.title: Design the guided programme conversation
steps.summary: This step designs the conversation that helps colleagues make sense of the evidence together. The conversation should help programme teams move from what they already know about their programme, to what the evidence adds, to what they may want to strengthen next. It should support academic judgement, not replace it.

outputs.title: Guided programme conversation structure
outputs.summary: A clear structure for moving from evidence, to interpretation, to programme story, to possible action.

outputs.title: Discussion prompts
outputs.summary: A small set of prompts to help programme teams explore patterns, strengths, questions and uncertainties in the evidence.

outputs.title: Facilitation notes
outputs.summary: Notes for faculty, department or programme colleagues who may lead the conversation locally.

outputs.title: Programme reflection and profile template
outputs.summary: A light template for capturing programme reflections, possible actions and useful examples or stories that emerge.

outputs.title: Responsible-use guidance
outputs.summary: Guidance on uncertainty, small numbers, sensitive comparisons and differences between disciplinary contexts.

existingCapacity.contribution: Provide input on academic development, facilitation, proportionate programme enhancement and the tone of the guided conversation.
Support interpretation of graduate futures evidence and responsible-use principles.
Test whether the conversation structure is credible and realistic in local programme contexts.
Provide feedback on whether the materials support good judgement without creating a heavy process.

decisions.title: Confirm purpose of the conversation
decisions.summary: Agree what a useful programme conversation should produce.

decisions.title: Confirm facilitation route
decisions.summary: Agree who is best placed to facilitate conversations locally.

decisions.title: Confirm level of structure
decisions.summary: Agree how much structure is helpful without making the conversation feel scripted.

decisions.title: Confirm capture method
decisions.summary: Agree how programme reflections, actions and story material should be captured without creating unnecessary burden.

decisions.title: Confirm follow-up expectation
decisions.summary: Agree what should happen after a programme has had the conversation.

risks.title: The conversation feels diagnostic or corrective
risks.mitigation: Design prompts around programme story, evidence, questions and action, not deficit identification.

risks.title: The materials become too heavy
risks.mitigation: Use a light core structure, with optional deeper prompts where needed.

risks.title: The model does not fit different disciplines
risks.mitigation: Test with colleagues from varied programme contexts and leave space for local interpretation.

steps.title: Work with early programme partners to refine the approach
steps.summary: This step works with a small number of early programme partners to try the pack and conversation model in real programme settings. These colleagues help us understand whether the approach feels useful, fair and proportionate, and whether it helps programmes surface their distinctive value as well as areas for further attention.

outputs.title: Early programme conversations
outputs.summary: A small number of real programme conversations using the evidence pack and guided conversation structure.

outputs.title: Feedback from academic colleagues
outputs.summary: Feedback on the pack, prompts, facilitation approach and overall usefulness of the process.

outputs.title: Examples of programme insight and story
outputs.summary: Examples of the kinds of insights, questions, possible actions and graduate futures stories the approach can generate.

outputs.title: Learning record
outputs.summary: A record of where the evidence was useful, where it was unclear and where further explanation or support was needed.

outputs.title: Recommended changes
outputs.summary: Changes to the pack, guidance and conversation model before wider use.

existingCapacity.contribution: Use the pack and conversation model in real programme settings and help shape the approach.
Support or host early conversations.
Interpret what is learned and identify changes needed before wider use.
Review whether the conversation model feels developmental, proportionate and academically credible where useful.

nonCashNeeds.need: Debrief and learning time after each early conversation
nonCashNeeds.rationale: The value of this step is in learning carefully from early use, not simply in completing the conversations.

decisions.title: Confirm early programme partners
decisions.summary: Agree which programmes should act as early partners.

decisions.title: Confirm invitation and briefing approach
decisions.summary: Agree how early partners should be invited and briefed so the work feels collegiate and constructive.

decisions.title: Confirm learning mix
decisions.summary: Agree what mix of programme types, disciplines and student profiles would give the most useful learning.

decisions.title: Confirm readiness for wider use
decisions.summary: Agree what evidence from early use is enough to support wider use.

decisions.title: Confirm how findings are shared
decisions.summary: Agree how learning can be shared without exposing, labelling or over-interpreting individual programmes.

risks.title: Early partners are read as being judged
risks.mitigation: Choose and invite partners for learning value, not because they are seen as problems.

risks.title: Early examples imply a single correct model
risks.mitigation: Use examples to show variation, not prescribe one programme story.

risks.title: The approach works only in unusually engaged contexts
risks.mitigation: Select early partners that provide useful variation in discipline, student profile and programme context.

steps.title: Improve the pack and conversation model from early use
steps.summary: This step uses what early programme partners tell us to make the pack and conversation model clearer, lighter and more useful. It is a chance to improve the evidence, the prompts, the guidance and the profile materials before wider programme engagement begins.

outputs.title: Improved graduate futures evidence pack
outputs.summary: A revised pack that better supports programme story, responsible interpretation and practical reflection.

outputs.title: Updated interpretation guidance
outputs.summary: Clearer guidance on responsible use, caveats and points that need better explanation.

outputs.title: Refined guided conversation structure
outputs.summary: Revised prompts and facilitation notes based on early programme use.

outputs.title: Clearer programme reflection and profile template
outputs.summary: A lighter template for capturing programme story, reflections, possible actions and useful examples.

outputs.title: Plan for the next programme groups
outputs.summary: A clear plan for the next group of programmes to work with, including the rationale for why those groups should come next.

existingCapacity.contribution: Make changes to the pack in response to early use.
Refine interpretation guidance and responsible-use principles.
Support refinements to conversation materials where the facilitation model needs adjustment.

existingCapacity.role: Faculty colleagues and early programme partners
existingCapacity.contribution: Confirm whether the revised approach is workable, proportionate and credible.

nonCashNeeds.rationale: Needed to prepare the next phase and avoid the work becoming too heavy for faculties or programme teams.

decisions.title: Confirm essential changes
decisions.summary: Agree which changes are essential before wider use and which can wait.

decisions.title: Confirm standard and flexible elements
decisions.summary: Agree which parts of the approach should be consistent across King's and which can flex locally.

decisions.title: Confirm next programme groups
decisions.summary: Agree which programme groups should be invited into the next phase.

decisions.title: Confirm rationale language
decisions.summary: Agree how to explain the rationale for the next programme groups in a fair and constructive way.

decisions.title: Confirm deeper conversation triggers
decisions.summary: Agree what level of evidence, opportunity or concern should lead to a deeper programme conversation.

risks.title: The model is locked too early
risks.mitigation: Use early feedback to make real changes rather than simply confirm the original design.

risks.title: Local flexibility makes the approach incoherent
risks.mitigation: Define a light common core with space for local adaptation.

risks.title: The next phase feels like targeting
risks.mitigation: Explain the rationale as focused support, story development and institutional learning.

steps.title: Work with agreed programme groups where the evidence can be most useful
steps.summary: This step works with agreed programme groups through a shared and transparent process. The evidence helps identify where a deeper conversation may be valuable, but faculty knowledge and programme context matter just as much. The purpose is to help colleagues strengthen their graduate futures story and identify practical next steps, not to label programmes.

outputs.title: Shared prioritisation approach
outputs.summary: An agreed approach for selecting programme groups, combining evidence, faculty knowledge, programme context and local judgement.

outputs.title: Guided programme conversations
outputs.summary: Supported conversations with agreed programme groups using the improved pack and conversation model.

outputs.title: Programme reflections
outputs.summary: Reflections on graduate futures strengths, questions, opportunities and support needs.

outputs.title: Practical local actions
outputs.summary: A small number of actions that programme teams can take forward locally.

outputs.title: Examples of distinctive programme value
outputs.summary: Evidence-informed examples of effective practice or distinctive graduate futures stories, where programme teams are comfortable for these to be shared.

outputs.title: Faculty-level learning
outputs.summary: Common themes, recurring questions and areas where further support may be needed.

existingCapacity.contribution: Lead the shared prioritisation process and agree the programme groups with faculties.
Host and facilitate programme conversations locally.
Support deeper interpretation where needed.
Engage with the evidence, own reflections and identify practical next steps.
Respond to questions raised by programme teams where feasible.

nonCashNeeds.rationale: Needed to keep the work manageable, avoid duplication and bring learning together without creating unnecessary reporting burden.

decisions.title: Confirm prioritisation criteria
decisions.summary: Agree what criteria should inform programme group selection.

decisions.title: Confirm who agrees programme groups
decisions.summary: Agree who needs to be involved so prioritisation combines evidence, faculty knowledge and local judgement.

decisions.title: Confirm rationale for inclusion
decisions.summary: Agree how the rationale for inclusion will be explained to colleagues.

decisions.title: Confirm support offer
decisions.summary: Agree what support programme teams can expect before, during and after the conversation.

decisions.title: Confirm theme capture
decisions.summary: Agree how faculty and institutional themes should be captured without reducing programmes to categories.

risks.title: Prioritisation feels punitive
risks.mitigation: Use a transparent shared process and include strengths, distinctive practice and learning value as well as support needs.

risks.title: Programme groups feel labelled
risks.mitigation: Frame the work as focused support for programme development and story strengthening.

risks.title: Programme actions become performative paperwork
risks.mitigation: Keep actions small, owned and routed through existing programme, department or faculty routes.

steps.title: Make the evidence pack a living resource
steps.summary: This step makes sure the evidence pack remains useful after the first phase of work. It agrees who will maintain it, how the evidence will be refreshed, how colleagues will get support with interpretation, and how graduate futures evidence will connect to ongoing quality assurance and continual improvement.

outputs.title: Evidence refresh rhythm
outputs.summary: An agreed rhythm for refreshing the evidence pack, including which data can be updated regularly and which data needs longer cycles.

outputs.title: Clear ownership model
outputs.summary: Ownership for maintaining the data, dashboard, narrative guidance and interpretation materials.

outputs.title: Update route
outputs.summary: A route for updating the pack as data sources, student patterns, programme needs or institutional priorities change.

outputs.title: Place in academic planning
outputs.summary: An agreed place for the pack in existing programme, department, faculty or institutional planning conversations.

outputs.title: Quality assurance and continual improvement handoff
outputs.summary: A clear route for graduate futures evidence to inform programme reflection and enhancement through existing cycles.

outputs.title: Ongoing feedback process
outputs.summary: A simple process for gathering feedback from colleagues who use the pack over time.

existingCapacity.contribution: Own or co-own the ongoing evidence resource, interpretation guidance and refresh route.
Maintain the dashboard, data updates and pack access route.
Agree how the pack will be used locally.

existingCapacity.role: Quality assurance and continual improvement routes
existingCapacity.contribution: Provide the institutional home for continued use of graduate futures evidence in programme reflection and enhancement.

enablingConditions.condition: A clear institutional home for the evidence pack after the initial project phase.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The pack risks becoming a one-off output rather than a sustained part of how King's understands graduate futures.

enablingConditions.condition: Agreement on how graduate futures evidence connects to existing quality assurance and continual improvement cycles.
enablingConditions.riskIfMissing: The evidence may remain available but not meaningfully shape programme enhancement.

decisions.title: Confirm ongoing owner
decisions.summary: Agree who owns the evidence pack after the initial project phase.

decisions.title: Confirm refresh rhythm
decisions.summary: Agree how often the evidence should be refreshed.

decisions.title: Confirm interpretation ownership
decisions.summary: Agree who owns the interpretation guidance and responsible-use principles.

decisions.title: Confirm central and local responsibilities
decisions.summary: Agree which parts of the pack need central maintenance and which can be shaped locally.

decisions.title: Confirm quality assurance and continual improvement route
decisions.summary: Agree where the pack sits in existing quality assurance, planning, review or enhancement cycles.

decisions.title: Confirm support route
decisions.summary: Agree what support is available when colleagues need help interpreting the evidence.

risks.title: The pack becomes a static dashboard
risks.mitigation: Confirm ownership for data, visualisation, narrative guidance and interpretation, not just dashboard refresh.

risks.title: The handoff is only technical
risks.mitigation: Include both technical ownership of the pack and academic ownership of how it is used.

risks.title: The pack creates a parallel process
risks.mitigation: Embed it in existing quality assurance, planning and enhancement cycles.

steps.title: Enable every programme to review and strengthen its graduate futures evidence
steps.summary: This step makes sure every agreed programme has a meaningful way to engage with its graduate futures evidence. Some programmes will have a supported conversation, while others will be enabled to self-review using the pack and guidance. The aim is that every programme can understand, evidence and strengthen its graduate futures story, and that King's can learn from the patterns emerging across programmes.

outputs.title: Supported reviews for remaining agreed programme groups
outputs.summary: Supported graduate futures conversations where direct support is needed.

outputs.title: Self-review route
outputs.summary: A route for programmes able to use the evidence pack, guidance and conversation materials without a supported conversation.

outputs.title: Review or enabled self-review confirmation
outputs.summary: Confirmation that every agreed programme has either completed a supported review or has been enabled to complete a self-review.

outputs.title: Institutional graduate futures synthesis
outputs.summary: A synthesis of themes across programmes, departments and faculties, including strengths, recurring questions and areas where further support may be needed.

outputs.title: Recommendations for related King's Edge work
outputs.summary: Recommendations for curriculum enhancement, skills, opportunity, evidence and student-facing story work.

existingCapacity.contribution: Support remaining programme conversations where direct support is needed.
Use the supported or self-review route to understand, evidence and strengthen their graduate futures story.
Support interpretation and synthesis.
Maintain the evidence pack and respond to feasible data questions.
Lead institutional synthesis and feed-through into related King's Edge work.

nonCashNeeds.rationale: Needed to track supported review and enabled self-review, bring findings together and avoid turning the final phase into a compliance exercise.

decisions.title: Confirm agreed programme population
decisions.summary: Agree what counts as an agreed programme for the purpose of this phase.

decisions.title: Confirm supported review and self-review routes
decisions.summary: Agree which programmes need a supported conversation and which can use the self-review route.

decisions.title: Confirm enabled self-review threshold
decisions.summary: Agree the minimum evidence needed to confirm that a programme has been enabled to self-review.

decisions.title: Confirm synthesis destination
decisions.summary: Agree where institutional themes and recommendations should go after this deliverable.

decisions.title: Confirm consent for visible examples
decisions.summary: Agree how programme ownership and consent will be handled where examples or stories are shared in student-facing, applicant-facing or recruitment-facing contexts.

risks.title: The final phase becomes a coverage exercise
risks.mitigation: Emphasise meaningful engagement with graduate futures evidence, with different routes depending on context, need and capacity.

risks.title: Self-review becomes abandonment
risks.mitigation: Provide clear materials, interpretation guidance and a route for help where evidence is unclear or sensitive.

risks.title: Synthesis flattens disciplinary difference
risks.mitigation: Draw out themes and examples while preserving context and avoiding categories or league tables.
